task.title: Relate task evidence to parent attempts
task.objective: Complete issue https://github.com/Knosence9/project-vela/issues/519 by adding a backward-compatible optional parent-attempt relation directly to task observations, with validation owned by the task aggregate and no separate execution-episode aggregate.
acceptance_criteria: Diagnostics, corrections, and verifications can reference one earlier attempt in the same task, and the relation survives reload.
Missing parents, non-attempt parents, and parented attempts are rejected before append and during replay of invalid version-two histories.
Existing ungrouped observations remain supported and payload-version-one observations replay without a parent or migration.
Independent review, hosted CodeRabbit review, required Rust quality gates, squash merge, and main synchronization complete successfully.

attempts.summary: Resolved the architecture boundary and opened a bounded execution issue.
attempts.diagnostic: The kernel already persisted ordered typed observations but could not group diagnostics, corrections, or verifications with the attempt they described. A separate episode aggregate would add ownership and lifecycle machinery before it was needed.
attempts.patch: Accepted task aggregate ownership in issue 518 and specified issue 519: add one optional parent attempt ID directly to observations, retain ungrouped evidence, validate append and replay, version the event payload, and defer automatic runtime evidence production.

attempts.summary: Demonstrated RED with parent-relation acceptance tests.
attempts.diagnostic: The focused observation target failed to compile with ten errors because TaskObservation had no parent accessor, TaskStore had no related-observation append API, and TaskStoreError had no typed parent-relation variants.
attempts.patch: Kept the failing persistence and rejection tests as executable contract evidence before changing production code.

attempts.summary: Implemented versioned parent-attempt persistence and validation.
attempts.diagnostic: New observation writes use payload version two with an optional parent_attempt_id. The existing append_observation API remains the ungrouped path, while append_observation_for_attempt validates that non-attempt evidence references an earlier same-task Attempt. Replay applies the same cardinality and kind rules; legacy version-one payloads decode as ungrouped.
attempts.patch: Updated crates/vela-kernel/src/task.rs, expanded task_observations integration coverage for every relation kind and failure mode, and documented aggregate ownership, compatibility, errors, durable shape, and non-goals.

attempts.summary: Strengthened replay-validation evidence after independent review.
attempts.diagnostic: A read-only Codex review questioned whether the blank parent-ID fixture proved typed ID validation rather than generic JSON rejection. The Rust raw byte string was valid JSON, but the broad MalformedPayload match did not make that distinction visible.
attempts.patch: Changed the test to assert the exact replay diagnostic, task observation id must not be blank, proving the payload reached TaskObservationId validation. The focused test and full gate passed afterward.

attempts.summary: Completed hosted verification, squash merge, and main synchronization.
attempts.diagnostic: The pinned-Nix gate passed formatting, workspace checks, 144 tests, Clippy with warnings denied, SecretSpec integration, and diff checks. Rust quality gate run 29672490185 passed. CodeRabbit passed all pre-merge checks and left one trivial DRY nitpick about the small append/replay validation helpers; it reported no blocking correctness finding.
attempts.patch: Squash auto-merge produced 7663c23e57b7a37afa36a292ac5159c4a6fd19b8, closed issue 519, and local main fast-forwarded to the merge. The non-blocking helper-consolidation suggestion was recorded as maintainability evidence rather than expanded into speculative post-merge behavior work.

outcome.summary: Vela task evidence can now form backward-compatible execution episodes by relating diagnostic, correction, and verification observations to one earlier attempt, while preserving ungrouped histories and rejecting invalid durable relations.

verification.command: /run/current-system/sw/bin/nix develop --command cargo test -p vela-kernel --test task_observations # 14 passed, 0 failed
/run/current-system/sw/bin/nix develop --command just verify # formatting, workspace checks, 144 tests, Clippy, SecretSpec, and diff gates passed

/run/current-system/sw/bin/nix develop --command codex exec --sandbox read-only 'Review the current uncommitted git diff for issue #519. Requirements: optional parent-attempt relation directly on task observations; version-one observation events replay ungrouped; all new writes use version two; attempts cannot have parents; a parent must be an earlier attempt in the same task; validate append and replay; typed append errors; preserve the ungrouped API; docs and tests. Report only blocking or important correctness, compatibility, API, or test findings. Do not modify files.' # one test-specific concern addressed with an exact diagnostic assertion; no production blocker
GitHub Rust quality gate https://github.com/Knosence9/project-vela/actions/runs/29672490185 and CodeRabbit review at https://github.com/Knosence9/project-vela/pull/520

lessons: An optional relation on the observation event owned by the task aggregate is enough to form coherent execution episodes without introducing a second aggregate or migration.
Preserving append_observation as the ungrouped API gives source compatibility and an explicit opt-in path for grouped evidence.
Event payload evolution should write only the new version while decoding the old version into a deliberate compatibility default.
Append and replay must enforce the same parent cardinality, ordering, and kind invariants so corrupt histories fail closed instead of producing ambiguous evidence.
A test that matches a broad malformed-payload category can hide which validation layer failed; asserting the exact typed diagnostic provides stronger evidence.
Small duplicated predicates can be acceptable inside a bounded slice, but review should record divergence risk so a future change can consolidate them when that work is independently justified.
